title: Meekerdin-maa
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Otheruses|Maa}} {{MultipleIssues|expand|updatecontent={{Update|}}}} {{Character |type=Criminal |image=[[File:Ratty.jpg]] |name=Meekerdin-Maa |homeworld= |birth=[[72 BBY/Legends|72 BBY]][[Dark Times 1|''Dark Times'' 1]] |death= |species=[[Tintinna/Legends|Tintinna]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=[[Brown]] |feathers= |eyes=Brown |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=''[[Uhumele]]'' |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|I'm not a youngling, you know. I'm fifty three.|Ratty|}} '''Meekerdin-maa''', also known as '''Ratty''', was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Tintinna/Legends|Tintinna]] who worked as the [[Engineer/Legends|engineer]] of the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Starship/Legends|vessel]] ''[[Uhumele]]''. He had an assistant named [[Janks]]. ==History== ===New Plympto=== Meekerdin-maa was with the rest of the crew when [[Dass Jennir]] and [[Bomo Greenbark]] snuck on-board the [[Uhumele]]. He informed them that they were the only survivors of the [[Cleansing of New Plympto#One_month_of_fighting|Battle of Half-Axe Pass]]. When Bomo demanded that they be taken to [[Sullust/Legends|Sullust]] to meet up with his family, Meekerdin-maa had to tell him that all the refugees were loaded on a ship to be sold to [[Slavery/Legends|slavers]]. He was busy in the engine room of the ''Uhumele'' during the frantic escape from New Plympto.[[Dark Times 2|''Dark Times'' 2]] ===Chasing after the Greenbark family=== ====In transit==== {{Quote|No. I know what loss is and I pledge myself to your cause, even if if leads to my death|Meekerdin-maa vowing to help [[Bomo Greenbark]] find his family|}} On their way to [[Orvax IV]], Meekerdin-maa comforted Bomo when he was informed by [[Mezgraf]] that it was a living hell of the [[Galaxy/Legends|Galaxy]]. When a quorum of the crew was called to decide what to do when they arrived at Orvax IV, he vowed to do everything in his power to help him find his family. ====Orvax IV==== {{Quote|Jennir said for us to keep you here!|Meekerdin-maa trying to stop [[Bomo Greenbark]] from leaving the ''Uhumele''|}} Upon [[Mission to Orvax IV|landing on Orvax IV]], Bomo and Meekerdin-maa disguised themselves as [[Jawa/Legends|Jawas]] with lights under their hoods to mimic the glowing yellow eyes. After Mezgraf wrangled a runaway pack animal and [[Dass Jennir]] used a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Mind trick/Legends|Mind trick]] to fool a pair of [[T'surr (species)|T'surr]] guards, Meekerdin-maa discovered a large group of [[Nosaurian/Legends|Nosaurians]] held in a lower level holding pit. As the crew of the [[Uhumele]] rushed to the pit, they were discovered by several T'surr guards. After Mezgraf, Jennir, [[Ko Vakier]], and Bomo dispatched them, most of the crew decided to return to the ''Uhumele''. Before leaving, Meekerdin-maa whispered to Jennir, "[[May the Force be with you/Legends|May the Force be with you]].." Upon returning to the ''Uhumele'', Bomo attempted to leave the ship to continue his search for his family, despite Meekerdin-maa's best attempts to contain him. He was with Bomo when Jennir returned with the coordinates to the buyer of [[Mesa Greenbark|Bomo's daughter]]. ====Esseles==== Once they arrived at [[Calamar]], Meekerdin-maa joined the rest of the crew in a meal while Jennir did some more investigating on the location of the owner of Bomo's daughter. When Jennir returned, they flew the ''Uhumele'' to [[Dezono Qua]]'s [[Qua family's villa|villa]] to [[Assault on Dezono Qua's villa|confront]] him. Meekerdin-maa, Bomo and [[Janks]] went to sabotage the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] control center. They successfully deactivated all the droids protecting Dezono. After Jennir killed Dezono when learning that Dezono killed and ate Bomo's daughter, Meekerdin-maa returned to the ''Uhumele'' and attempted to comfort Bomo. ===Trying to find a deal for the [[Dreypa's Oubliette|artifact]]=== ====Pizkoss==== {{Quote|I knew I should have switched out the ''Uhumele's'' transponder after Orvax|Meekerdin-maa's comment to Bomo while running from Stormtroopers|[[Dark Times 6|''Dark Times'' 6]]}} While on [[Pizkoss]], Meekerdin-maa and Bomo were chased by [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]]. After hiding under a market table, they ran into [[Janks]], spilling all the food he had purchased. Before they could gather it up and escape, they were discovered by more stormtroopers. Bomo started firing at them, but Meekerdin-maa grabbed his arm and told him to run. They fled, leaving Janks behind to be captured. Before reaching the docking bay where the ''[[Uhumele]]'' was, Meekerdin-maa called [[Crys Taanzer]] on his [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] to warn her of Imperial trouble. Upon entering the [[Hangar/Legends|docking bay]], Meekerdin-maa and Bomo found three stormtroopers setting up an [[E-Web heavy repeating blaster/Legends|E-Web heavy repeating blaster]]. Crys lifted off, scattering the stormtroopers while lowering a rope ladder for her crewmembers. Meekerdin-maa and Bomo were hauled aboard by [[Schurk-Heren]] and [[Mezgraf]]. Once safely aboard, Meekerdin-maa informed his captain that Janks was captured. ====Mimban==== {{Quote|At least they've stopped shooting at us…''"
"''That's because they have us, Meekerdin-maa''"
"''Oh.|Meekerdin-maa and Schurk-Heren|[[Dark Times 8|''Dark Times'' 8]]}} The crew of the ''Uhumele'' headed to Mimban for a rendouvous with a buyer for the mysterious artifact they possessed. Before they landed, the crew stopped in an [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid field]] to hide the artifact. Bomo volunteered to wear Meekerdin-maa's [[Environment suit/Legends|spacesuit]] and help, and Meekerdin-maa quickly went off to replace the ship's transponder. Once on Mimban, Meekerdin-maa and the rest of the crew met with [[Haka Hai]] at his base. When a firefight broke out after [[Lumbra]] doublecrossed Hara right after he tried to steal the artifact from Schurk-Heren, Meekerdin-maa sought cover with the rest of the crew of the ''Uhumele''. He was captured along with everyone else by Haka's gang after Lumbra fled with the artifact. Meekerdin-maa was chained to a large ball and forced to work on Haka's equipment. After Bomo escaped captivity, Meekerdin-maa observed Haka and his men heading into the swamp and assumed they were after Ko Vakier. He was freed by Bomo and Crys after they raided the armory. The engineer used his skills to rig up some [[Grenade/Legends|grenades]] to explode remotely so Bomo could use them in an ambush. He then went to free Schurk-Heren, Ko Vakier and Mezgraf from their cell. After Haka's gang was [[Skirmish on Mimban|wiped out]], Meekerdin-maa returned to the ''Uhumele'' with the rest of the surviving crew. ===Reunion with Dass Jennir=== Eventually, the crew of the ''Uhumele'' decided to assassinate Darth Vader, the second in command of Emperor Palpatine. Dass Jennir also had Meekerdin-maa secretly tasked with working on a project. When inquired Bomo Greenbark inquired about the purchases, Meekerdin-maa would dismiss this as merely things things ''Uhumele'' needed, although the subsequent discreet interactions with Jennir prompted suspicions. ==Personality and traits== As befitting any excellent engineer, Meekerdin-Maa was uncannily observant as well as extraordinarily sensitive, not only to the machinery under his care but with his companions, as well. Despite his age—fifty-three standard years—his personality and stature often led others to think of him as a [[Youngling/Legends|youngling]]. Though the end of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] led many peoples throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] to disdain the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and disbelieve [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], Meekerdin-Maa stood by his principles and believed in the Force, and generally thought well of its now-"extinct" servants. He was also very compassionate and cared deeply for his crewmates and any passengers. It was this caring nature that lead him to become good friends with Bomo. ==Appearances== {{ScrollBox|content= {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Meekerdin-maa}} *[[Dark Times 1|''Dark Times'' 1]] {{1st}} *[[Dark Times 2|''Dark Times'' 2]] *[[Dark Times 3|''Dark Times'' 3]] *[[Dark Times 4|''Dark Times'' 4]] *[[Dark Times 5|''Dark Times'' 5]] *[[Dark Times 6|''Dark Times'' 6]] *[[Dark Times 7|''Dark Times'' 7]] *[[Dark Times 8|''Dark Times'' 8]] *[[Dark Times 9|''Dark Times'' 9]] *[[Dark Times 10|''Dark Times'' 10]] *[[Dark Times 11|''Dark Times'' 11]] *[[Dark Times 12|''Dark Times'' 12]] *[[Dark Times: Blue Harvest, Part 1|''Dark Times: Blue Harvest'', Part 1]] {{Flash}} *[[Dark Times 15|''Dark Times'' 15]] *[[Dark Times 16|''Dark Times'' 16]] *[[Dark Times 17|''Dark Times'' 17]] *[[Dark Times—Out of the Wilderness 1|''Dark Times—Out of the Wilderness'' 1]] *[[Dark Times—Out of the Wilderness 2|''Dark Times—Out of the Wilderness'' 2]] *[[Dark Times—Out of the Wilderness 3|''Dark Times—Out of the Wilderness'' 3]] *[[Dark Times—Out of the Wilderness 4|''Dark Times—Out of the Wilderness'' 4]] *[[Dark Times—A Spark Remains 1|''Dark Times—A Spark Remains'' 1]] *[[Dark Times—A Spark Remains 2|''Dark Times—A Spark Remains'' 2]] *[[Dark Times—A Spark Remains 4|''Dark Times—A Spark Remains'' 4]] {{Flash}} }} ==Sources== *{{InsiderCite|90|Drawn by the Force}} {{Imo}} *''[[Star Wars: Panel to Panel Volume 2: Expanding the Universe]]'' {{Po}} *{{InsiderCite|95|Jawa's Corner}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Meekerdin-Maa |pl=Meekerdin-maa }} [[Category:Engineers]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Smugglers]] [[Category:Tintinna]]
